title: trientalis europaea subsp. arctica
intro: Trientalis europaea subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic wintergreen, is a sub-species of a low-growing perennial plant native to the Arctic regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and its potential to reduce pain and fever. The health benefits of Arctic wintergreen include its ability to help alleviate digestive issues, soothe respiratory problems, and support the immune system. Traditionally, Arctic wintergreen has been prepared as a tea or infused in oil, and its extracts have also been used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
